items.summary: This pull request introduces a significant technical update to the openclaw project by adding CLI provider support for text-to-speech (TTS). It addresses an issue where users relied solely on cloud TTS services, which could compromise privacy and increase latency. The update enables local TTS tools (like espeak, piper, mlx-audio) to be used directly within the system, enhancing privacy by keeping data on-device and supporting offline operation. The implementation includes support for template variables, environment variable passing, and improved configuration flexibility. Overall, this change improves performance, privacy, and usability by integrating local TTS capabilities into the platform.

items.summary: The GitHub issue reports a regression introduced in openclaw version 2026.3.11 where cron jobs configured with `sessionTarget: "isolated"` are enqueued successfully but fail to execute. Despite being scheduled and triggered manually, these jobs do not run, indicating a potential bug affecting isolated session job execution post-upgrade. The issue is documented on the openclaw repository, with steps to reproduce involving creating and manually running an isolated session cron job. The problem impacts users on macOS 15.7.4 using Node.js v22.22.0. The issue remains open, highlighting a need for a bug fix or performance fix in the upcoming releases.

items.summary: The GitHub issue reports a bug in the openclaw/openclaw project where the `Edit` tool unexpectedly deletes the entire file content, reducing it to 0 bytes, despite correctly matching the old text and indicating success. This problem occurs during in-place text replacements, specifically in the environment using OpenClaw v2026.3.11 within a Docker sandbox on Linux. The bug affects file editing operations, leading to data loss rather than content modification. No mention of related pull requests or performance improvements is provided.

items.title: [Bug]: isolated cron job sessions with kimi-coding/k2p5 as a model can't use tools · Issue #44269 · openclaw/openclaw
highlights:
44269 - Repository: openclaw/openclaw | Your own personal AI assistant. Any OS. Any Platform. The lobster way. 🦞 | 309K stars | TypeScript ## [Bug]: isolated cron job sessions with kimi-coding/k2p5 as a model can't use tools - Author: @TchelloSimis
- State: open
- Labels: bug, regression
- Created: 2026-03-12T17:28:47Z - Updated: 2026-03-13T00:54:12Z ### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary Ever since 2026.3.2, isolated cron job sessions with kimi-coding/k2p5 as a model can't use tools. This means it can't access thew workspace and do any types of tasks. It seems to be exclusive to Kimi models. The issue perisists even in more recent releases. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create a mock text file at /home/clawdbot/.openclaw/workspace/. I created a file called fruits.txt, with contents:  ...    `openclaw cron create --name "test-fruit" --at "+3m" --model kimi-coding/k2p5 --session isolated --channel telegram --to "CHAT_ID" --message "Read the contents of /home/clawdbot/.openclaw/workspace/fruit.txt and output the full  ...    1. Wait for execution

### Expected behavior

Agent outputs

```
mango
pineapple
kiwi
pomegranate
```  ...    ```
apple
banana
cherry
dragonfruit
elderberry
```

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.11

### Operating system Ubuntu 24.04

### Install method

_No response_

### Model

kimi-coding/k2p5

### Provider / routing chain openclaw -> kimi-coding -> kimi

### Config file / key location

_No response_  ...    ### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

The fact that it's Kimi-specific suggests either: > > 1. Tool schema incompatibility between kimi-coding/k2p5 and isolated cron bootstrap (e.g., `requiresOpenAiAnthropicToolPayload` or tool format mismatch) > 2. Isolated cron's system prompt doesn't include tool definitions for Kimi (unlikely but check) > 3. A v2026.3.2+ change in how Kimi models are initialized that breaks tool availability
>  ...    > > I use Kimi as my main model. It had issues when working as the main agent, but the issues were fixed with the 2026.3.11 update. The issues for isolated sessions persist. I know that the issue is specific to isolated sessions; I  ...    > same. i just updated to 3-11 and:
> in discord channels - tool callign works.  ...    > Same issue here with `kimi-coding/k2p5` + isolated cron sessions.

**@poori** commented · Mar 12, 2026 at 6:30pm > since Kimi is considered the third best option after anthropic and openai, a lot of ppl seem affected by this but are not here in the github issues. > 
> https://www.reddit.com/r/kimi/comments/1rq9gcl/comment/o9wzrix/  ...    > Same issue here with kimi-coding/k2p
items.summary: This GitHub issue reports a regression introduced in openclaw version 2026.3.2, where isolated cron job sessions using the kimi-coding/k2p5 model can no longer access tools or the workspace. Despite previous functionality, these sessions are now unable to perform tasks such as reading files, which impacts automation workflows. The problem appears specific to Kimi models and persists across recent releases. To reproduce, users create a cron job that attempts to read a file in the workspace, which fails due to the tool access restriction. The issue is ongoing and affects automation and scripting capabilities within openclaw when using Kimi models.

items.summary: This pull request (PR #44016) for the openclaw/openclaw repository updates the default runtime environment to Node.js v24 (LTS), ensuring compatibility with Node v22.16+ versions. The change involves modifications across 39 files, with 151 additions and 108 deletions, aimed at streamlining the runtime environment for better performance and future-proofing. The update was merged on March 12, 2026, by contributor @obviyus, following review and approval. This technical update enhances stability and compatibility, addressing potential issues related to runtime version discrepancies and optimizing the application's performance with the latest LTS release.

items.title: macOS Release - OpenClaw
highlights:
the complete documentation index at: https://docs.openclaw.ai/llms.txt  ...    # macOS Release

# OpenClaw macOS release (Sparkle) This app now ships Sparkle auto-updates. Release builds must be Developer ID–signed, zipped, and published with a  ...    ## Prereqs - Sparkle tools are fetched automatically via SwiftPM at `apps/macos/.build/artifacts/sparkle/Sparkle/bin/` (`sign_update`, `generate_appcast`, etc.).  ...    - `xcrun notarytool store-credentials "openclaw-notary" --key /tmp/openclaw-notary.p8 --key-id "$APP_STORE_CONNECT_KEY_ID" --issuer "$APP_STORE_CONNECT_ISSUER_ID"` - `echo "$APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_P8" | sed's/\\n/\n/g' > /tmp/openclaw-notary.p8` - `APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_P8`, `APP_STORE_CONNECT_KEY_ID`, `APP_STORE_CONNECT_ISSUER_ID` - We use a Keychain profile named `openclaw-notary`, created from App Store Connect API key env vars in your shell  ...    - Sparkle private key path set in the environment as `SPARKLE_PRIVATE_KEY_FILE` (path to your Sparkle ed25519 private  ...    - Developer ID Application cert installed (example: `Developer ID Application: ()`). ## Build & package

Notes: - Use `scripts/package-mac-dist.sh` for release artifacts (zip + DMG + notarization). Use `scripts/package-mac-app.sh` for local/dev packaging. - For `BUILD_CONFIG=release`, `scripts/package-mac-app.sh` now defaults to universal (`arm64 x86_64`) automatically. You can still override with `BUILD_ARCHS=arm64` or `BUILD_ARCHS=x86_64`. For local/dev builds (`BUILD_CONFIG=debug`), it defaults to the current architecture (`$(uname -m)`). - You can still override `APP_BUILD` explicitly when release engineering needs a specific monotonic value. - If `APP_BUILD` is omitted, `scripts/package-mac-app.sh` derives a Sparkle-safe default from `APP_VERSION` (`YYYYMMDDNN`: stable defaults to `90`, prereleases use a suffix-derived lane) and uses the higher of that value and  ...    ```bash
# From repo root; set release IDs so Sparkle feed is enabled.  ...    SKIP_NOTARIZE=1 \
BUNDLE_ID=ai.openclaw.mac \
APP_VERSION=2026.3.11 \
BUILD_CONFIG=release \  ...    # `package-mac-dist.sh` already creates the zip + DMG. # If you used `package-mac-app.sh` directly instead, create them manually:  ...    ditto -c -k --sequesterRsrc --keepParent dist/OpenClaw.app dist/OpenClaw-2026.3.11.zip  ...    scripts/create-dmg.sh dist/OpenClaw.app dist/OpenClaw-2026.3.11.dmg  ...    #   xcrun notarytool store-credentials "openclaw-notary" \  ...    NOTARIZE=1 NOTARYTOOL_PROFILE=openclaw-notary \
BUNDLE_ID=ai.openclaw.mac \
APP_VERSION=2026.3.11 \  ...    ```

## Appcast entry

Use the release note generator so Sparkle renders formatted HTML notes:  ...    SPARKLE_PRIVATE_KEY_FILE=/path/to/ed25519-private-key scripts/make_appcast.sh dist/OpenClaw-2026.3.11.zip https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openclaw/openclaw/main/appcast.xml ``` Generates HTML release notes from `CHANGELOG.md` (via `scripts/changelog-to-html.sh`) and embeds them in the appcast entry. Commit the updated `appcast.xml` alongside the release assets (zip + dSYM) when publishing. ## Publish & verify - On a previous public build, run “Check for Updates…” from the About tab and verify Sparkle installs the new build  ...    - `curl -I https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openclaw/openclaw/main/appcast.xml` returns 200.
- Sanity checks:  ...    `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openclaw/openclaw/main/appcast.xml`. - Upload `OpenClaw-2026.3.11.zip` (and `OpenClaw-2026.3.11.dSYM.zip`) to the GitHub release for tag `v2026.3.11`. Definition of done: signed app + appcast are published, update flow works from an older installed version, and release assets are attached to the GitHub release. Built with Mintlify.
items.summary: The webpage provides documentation for the macOS release of OpenClaw, focusing on its Sparkle-based auto-update system. It details prerequisites such as fetching Sparkle tools via SwiftPM, installing dependencies with pnpm, and setting up notarization credentials with Apple. The release process requires signing builds with a Developer ID, creating signed appcast entries, and publishing updates accordingly. For comprehensive details, users are directed to the full documentation index. The page does not explicitly include API documentation, architecture specifics, release notes, or changelog details.

items.summary: This pull request addresses a bug in the OpenClaw project related to OAuth profile expiry display. Previously, the system incorrectly showed stale expiry information ("expires in 0m") for auto-renewable OAuth profiles, despite the status being overridden to "ok" after a refresh. The fix involves clearing the `expiresAt` and `remainingMs` fields when profiles are set to auto-renew, ensuring that downstream renderers accurately reflect the profile's active status without displaying misleading expiry data. This update improves the correctness of OAuth profile status indicators, preventing confusion caused by outdated expiry information.

items.summary: This pull request introduces a significant technical update to the openclaw project by adding an iOS APNs relay gateway. It enhances the push notification system by integrating relay-backed APNs registration and delivery handling, specifically for iOS devices. The update includes new features such as iOS relay registration, App Attest support, and official-build push configurations. Additionally, the changes document the beta relay flow and address build unblock requirements for testing. Overall, this update improves the reliability and functionality of push notifications on iOS, representing a substantial enhancement rather than a bug fix or performance optimization.

items.summary: The webpage discusses an issue with the versioning rules of the openclaw plugin used in WeChat Work (企业微信). The user highlights a problem where the plugin's prerelease version (1.0.7-beta.1) is not installed by default because npm requires explicit request for prereleases. This causes confusion or errors when users attempt to install or update the plugin. The post references a GitHub issue where this problem is discussed, emphasizing that the versioning or release process may need adjustment to better handle prerelease versions, especially for developers and users involved in technical integration, architecture, or performance optimization.
